title: podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. forrestii
intro: Podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. forrestii, also known as the Forrester's Podocarpus, is a slow-growing, evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of southern China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Forrester's Podocarpus has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use.
